understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the currently selected piece (and any other selectable pieces) around the grid to ultimately reach/cover the goal tile(s) (+), likely by positioning a selectable onto a goal within the step limit.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions moved the selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, unless blocked by a wall or an immovable boundary/object. CLICK switched control to another selectable piece at the specified pixel coordinate (matching the positions listed in the state). When moving into a pushable object adjacent in the move direction, the object could be pushed along with the selected piece if space allowed; otherwise movement would fail. Walls/blocked edges prevented movement, though the 'blocked' indicator was only a hint and required trying moves to learn exact collision behavior.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls, but the heuristic can be wrong; try moving right to probe for any passable boundary or interaction.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls, but the heuristic can be wrong; try moving right to probe for any passable boundary or interaction.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | All adjacent directions are blocked by walls for the currently selected piece, so switch to the other selectable to test for an available move path toward the goal.
history.reasoning: All adjacent directions are blocked by walls for the currently selected piece, so switch to the other selectable to test for an available move path toward the goal.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_UP | We appear enclosed by walls on all four sides; test a move to confirm whether any wall boundary is passable/quirky.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls on all four sides; test a move to confirm whether any wall boundary is passable/quirky.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | This selectable is fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch back to the other selectable to continue exploring for a path to the goal.
history.reasoning: This selectable is fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch back to the other selectable to continue exploring for a path to the goal.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | All adjacent directions show walls, but we should probe for any non-standard wall behavior; try moving right first to see if it allows sliding/teleporting/interactions.
history.reasoning: All adjacent directions show walls, but we should probe for any non-standard wall behavior; try moving right first to see if it allows sliding/teleporting/interactions.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear boxed in by walls on all sides; try moving right into the adjacent selectable to see if pushing/switching behavior allows progress.
history.reasoning: We appear boxed in by walls on all sides; try moving right into the adjacent selectable to see if pushing/switching behavior allows progress.

history.summary: Turn 7: CLICK | This piece is completely enclosed by walls on all four sides, so we must switch to the other selectable to make progress toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: This piece is completely enclosed by walls on all four sides, so we must switch to the other selectable to make progress toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Test stepping onto/into the adjacent goal area to see if it triggers completion despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: Test stepping onto/into the adjacent goal area to see if it triggers completion despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_UP | Test whether moving onto/through the adjacent goal area is allowed despite the wall adjacency hint.
history.reasoning: Test whether moving onto/through the adjacent goal area is allowed despite the wall adjacency hint.
